source_pr.title: plan: fix a problem caused by union's schema
source_pr.body:
<!--
Thank you for contributing to TiDB! Please read TiDB's [CONTRIBUTING](https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md) document **BEFORE** filing this PR.
-->

### What problem does this PR solve? <!--add issue link with summary if exists-->

Before this commit. Union use the schema of its `Children[0]`.
The `Columns` information is correct.
But the `unique key information` is not, obviously.

### What is changed and how it works?

Give it a schema held by itself.

### Check List <!--REMOVE the items that are not applicable-->

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

 - Unit test

Code changes

 - Has exported variable/fields change
 - Has persistent data change

This is found by https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/pull/7676

symptoms: result = tk.MustQuery("select max(a) from t group by a order by a") (executor/aggregate_test.go)
failure_signature: result = tk.MustQuery("select max(a) from t group by a order by a") (executor/aggregate_test.go)
root_cause_explanation: The test was flaky because it relied on nondeterministic result ordering (e.g., missing ORDER BY / unsorted assertions). Result ordering is not guaranteed unless explicitly enforced. This PR stabilizes it by: Sort results / enforce deterministic ordering before assertions to avoid nondeterministic flakiness.
fix_pattern: Sort results / enforce deterministic ordering before assertions to avoid nondeterministic flakiness.

evidence: evidence: result = tk.MustQuery("select max(a) from t group by a order by a") (executor/aggregate_test.go)

files: cmd/explaintest/r/explain_complex.result, cmd/explaintest/r/explain_complex_stats.result, cmd/explaintest/r/explain_easy.result, executor/aggregate_test.go, plan/exhaust_physical_plans.go, plan/logical_plan_builder.go, plan/logical_plan_test.go, plan/logical_plans.go, plan/physical_plans.go, plan/rule_column_pruning.go, plan/rule_partition_processor.go
